1.title: Check your name on the Electoral Roll
1.plain_english: Before you can vote, your name has to be on your state's electoral roll. This is the official list of all eligible voters in your area. You can check online or visit your local election office.
how_to: Visit nvsp.in (National Voter Service Portal)
Click 'Search in Electoral Roll'
Enter your state and district
Search by name or Voter ID number
You'll see your entry if registered
common_mistakes: Spelling your name wrong (try alternate spellings)
Not including your middle name or initials
Expecting instant results (may take 24-48 hours after registration)

2.title: Register / Update your details
2.plain_english: If your name isn't on the electoral roll yet, you need to register. You can do this online or by filing Form 6 at your local election office. If your details have changed, file Form 8 to update them.
how_to: Visit nvsp.in and click 'Register as a Voter'
Choose 'New Voter Registration'
Fill in your details: name, DOB, address, state
Upload proof of address (utility bill, rental agreement, etc.)
Upload proof of identity (Aadhaar, PAN, passport, etc.)
Submit and note your reference number
Your application will be reviewed in 7-10 days
common_mistakes: Using a residential address that doesn't match your documents

3.title: Track your application status
3.plain_english: After you register, you can track whether your application has been approved. Use the reference number you got when you applied.
how_to: Visit nvsp.in
Click 'Track Application Status'
Enter your reference number
Check the status: Pending, Approved, or Rejected
If rejected, it will show the reason
common_mistakes: Checking before 7-10 days (takes time to review)
Not checking the email or SMS updates from election office

4.title: Find your polling booth
4.plain_english: On election day, you'll vote at a specific polling booth (voting center) near your address. You can find which booth is yours by your voter ID or name.
how_to: Visit your state's election commission website
Search 'Polling Booth Locator' + your state
Enter your voter ID number or name
View your booth location, address, and timing
Note the booth name and timing (usually 7 AM to 6 PM)
Plan to arrive early to avoid lines
common_mistakes: Going to the wrong polling booth
Not bringing required ID on election day
Assuming you already know where your booth is

5.title: Vote on election day
5.plain_english: On election day, go to your polling booth with valid ID, press the button next to your choice, and get your VVPAT confirmation. Your vote is secret and your choice is protected by law.
how_to: Bring a valid ID (Voter ID, Aadhaar, PAN, driving license, passport)
Go to your polling booth at the assigned time
Tell the officer your name and they'll verify your details
You'll be given a ballot paper or finger-marked
Go inside the voting booth (it's private and secret)
Press the button next to your choice
Verify your choice on the VVPAT slip
Your vote is now recorded
common_mistakes: Trying to vote at the wrong booth
Telling others who you voted for (vote is secret)
Leaving after pressing button without verifying VVPAT
